subject: Geography
title: The Durand Line
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Durand Line usable.

hook.text: In 1893, a British diplomat drew a line through Pashtun tribal lands with a single meeting.

buildup.text: The Durand Line became the border between Afghanistan and British India, now Pakistan.

discovery.text: It split the Pashtun people—the world's largest tribal group—across two countries.

twist.text: Afghanistan has never accepted the border. Pakistan insists it is permanent.

climax.text: This 130-year-old line drawn by a foreign power remains one of the world's most dangerous borders.

punchline.text: One afternoon's diplomacy created a century of conflict.

quiz.question: What people were split by the Durand Line?
